name: Engineering
domain: Stress/strain, circuit laws, control theory, structural mechanics
core_insight: Physical systems respond linearly near equilibrium. Feedback loops are the core design tool: PID control corrects error proportionally, accumulatively, and predictively. Structure fails at the critical load where elastic response yields to plastic deformation.

key_equations.name: PID Controller
key_equations.why: Universal feedback law: proportional corrects now, integral corrects drift, derivative anticipates

key_equations.name: Hooke's Law
key_equations.why: Stress proportional to strain in elastic regime — the linear response foundation of all structural analysis

key_equations.name: Ohm's Law
key_equations.why: Voltage, current, resistance: the circuit analog of all linear dissipative systems

seed_prompt: ⚙ Where is the error? Build the feedback loop. Elastic now — how close to yield?
